name: Metabase
description: Open source business intelligence and analytics platform. Create dashboards and explore data without writing SQL.
summary: Metabase is actively releasing across multiple stable branches simultaneously. The latest stable release is v0.59.6 (Open Source) / v1.59.6 (Enterprise), released on April 9, 2025. Multiple concurrent patch releases across branches 54–59 were published on the same date, indicating active long-term support across versions. A beta for v0.60.0 was released on March 27, 2025. Full changelogs are available at https://github.com/metabase/metabase/releases.

answers.What is the current license?: Open Source edition uses AGPL v3; Enterprise edition requires a commercial license from Metabase.
answers.Is the project actively maintained?: Yes — multiple releases across several stable branches published in April 2025, with a v0.60.0-beta also available.
answers.Is there a cloud-hosted version availabl: Yes — Metabase Cloud (managed hosting) is available at metabase.com.
answers.Does it support SSO and SAML authenticat: Yes — SSO and SAML are supported in the Enterprise edition.
answers.What databases/data sources are supporte: PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, SQL Server, SQLite, MongoDB, Presto, Druid, Oracle, SparkSQL, and more (connector list not detailed in this changelog excerpt).

alternatives.name: Redash
alternatives.review: Open source, query-focused, simpler feature set.

alternatives.name: Apache Superset
alternatives.review: Apache-licensed, rich visualisation library, steeper setup curve.

alternatives.name: Grafana
alternatives.review: Primarily metrics/time-series but increasingly used for general BI.

alternatives.name: Hex
alternatives.review: Close source, enterprise focus, AI Analytics Platform for your whole team

alternatives.name: Tableau
alternatives.review: Offers far more advanced visualization and analytics capabilities than Metabase but is significantly more expensive and not open source.

alternatives.name: Lightdash
alternatives.review: Tightly integrated with dbt for a metrics-as-code workflow, making it more governed and developer-friendly than Metabase but less accessible to purely non-technical users.
